# Ortelius

> Ortelius is a Codex-facing workflow system for building typed/fibered graph
> JSON through graph-population protocol assets and Python validation /
> materialization tools.

Ortelius is used by pointing Codex at the graph-population protocol schema,
usually with a `MAKE-GRAPH` request. Codex then creates a generated protocol
bundle, records graph intent for broad domains, executes bounded graph-population
actions through repo-local Markdown and JSON control surfaces, and writes
candidate typed/fibered graph JSON.

The Python package does not call Codex or crawl sources by itself. It loads,
validates, inspects, and materializes graph JSON produced by the protocol
workflow.

For broad `MAKE-GRAPH` domains, require a graph-intent contract or explicit
infer-and-proceed authorization before treating source discovery, type discovery,
edge discovery, or record counts as graph-population progress.

## Core Context

- [README](README.md): Human quick start, badges, install commands, graph model,
  Codex usage, CLI examples, and generated-bundle policy.
- [Full LLM context](docs/llm_context.md): Compact project explanation,
  protocol summary, command examples, and file map for LLM/agent use.
- [Project metadata](pyproject.toml): Package name, version, Python
  requirement, dependencies, CLI entry point, pytest config, and ruff config.

## Protocols

- [Graph population protocol schema](assets/protocol_assets/system/graph_population/v001/graph_population_protocol_schema.md): Codex-facing compiler document for `MAKE-GRAPH` and `GENERATE-BUNDLE`.
- [Graph population control protocol](assets/protocol_assets/system/graph_population/v001/graph_population_control_protocol.md): Codex-facing executor document for `EXECUTE-BUNDLE`.
- [Protocol manifest](assets/protocol_assets/system/graph_population/v001/manifest.json): Machine-readable index for the v001 system protocol pair.
- [System protocol README](assets/protocol_assets/system/graph_population/v001/README.md): Short boundary and file map for the protocol asset directory.

## Examples And Fixtures

- [Generic type graph nodes](tests/fixtures/graph_assets/minimal_generic/graphs/tg/nodes.json): Minimal public type graph node fixture.
- [Generic type graph edges](tests/fixtures/graph_assets/minimal_generic/graphs/tg/edges.json): Minimal public type graph edge fixture.
- [Generic fiber graph nodes](tests/fixtures/graph_assets/minimal_generic/graphs/fg/nodes.json): Minimal public fiber graph node fixture.
- [Generic fiber graph edges](tests/fixtures/graph_assets/minimal_generic/graphs/fg/edges.json): Minimal public fiber graph edge fixture.
- [Minimal generated bundle fixture](tests/fixtures/protocol_assets/minimal_generated_bundle/): Test fixture for generated-bundle validation.

## Source

- [Python package](src/ortelius/): Loader, model, validator, protocol-asset
  checks, CLI, and graph framework materializers.
- [CLI implementation](src/ortelius/cli.py): `ortelius validate`, `inspect`,
  `materialize`, and `protocol` subcommands.
- [Tests](tests/): Unit, fixture, protocol, materialization, CLI, and README
  badge tests.

## Optional

- [CI workflow](.github/workflows/ci.yml): GitHub Actions workflow for tests,
  lint, protocol validation, graph validation, materialization smokes, and
  package build.
